name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Design

description: Initial eval design artifacts and scoring harness

asi.hypothesis: A compact manual-light eval with cited source evidence and tmux capture scripts will satisfy the design prompt while avoiding expensive automation.
asi.primary_gap: Exact Claude Code CLI release/source history not inspected; marked as a gap in eval-plan.md.

description: Add Claude Bridge Anthropic cache-stat capture fields

asi.hypothesis: Adding explicit claude-bridge cache creation/read token fields improves the eval's token-spend analysis for Anthropic models without changing scope or gaming the completeness scorer.
asi.learned: Primary score improves to 99 and the eval now captures cache behavior separately from raw prompt/completion tokens.

description: Add native child-session wiring citation

asi.hypothesis: A concrete citation for native child-session wiring improves evidence quality and reaches the completeness target without adding scope or automating expensive eval runs.
asi.learned: The eval now cites enough native/extension/lineage source paths to max the scorer while preserving the 8-scenario compact design.
asi.overfit_check: Change adds real evaluative evidence needed by the prompt; it is not just scorer padding because it identifies where parent services and active-tool ceilings enter runtime construction.

description: Add complete Claude Bridge cache accounting columns to scorecard

asi.hypothesis: Adding uncached input and output columns to the Claude Bridge cache accounting would improve practical eval usefulness while preserving the compact design.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at the current best/saturated score, so autoresearch rules require discard despite the qualitative improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: If cache accounting is strategically important, update the primary metric rubric to include cache-field completeness in a new config header; otherwise focus on finding correctness gaps rather than saturated completeness changes.

description: Mention Claude Bridge cache stats in README quickstart

asi.hypothesis: Surfacing Claude Bridge cache-stat capture in the quickstart would reduce operator misses during eval runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric was unchanged at the saturated best score, so autoresearch rules require discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Primary metric cannot improve further under the current 100-point completeness scorer; either reinitialize with a richer quality metric that includes cache/accounting completeness and clarity, or proceed to actual eval execution rather than artifact optimization.
asi.saturation_observation: eval_score is capped by 5 required files, 8 scenarios, 20 citations, and 2 scripts; current artifacts already satisfy all scorer dimensions.

description: Add uncached/output Claude Bridge cache fields to findings template

asi.hypothesis: Adding uncached input and output token fields to the findings template would make Claude Bridge Anthropic cache analysis complete in the final report.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric remained unchanged at the saturated 100 score, so autoresearch rules require discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep making qualitative-only artifact tweaks under this saturated metric. Most promising next step is to reinitialize the experiment with a richer primary metric that scores cache-accounting completeness, table validity, and runbook executability, or move from design optimization to actual A/B execution.
asi.saturation_observation: Current eval_score cannot exceed 100; best remains 100 with required_files=5, scenario_count=8, citation_count=20, script_count=2.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Quality

description: Switch scorer to richer quality metric including Claude Bridge cache accounting

asi.hypothesis: A richer primary metric should break saturation by scoring cache-accounting completeness for Anthropic models via claude-bridge.
asi.rollback_reason: Shell interpreted the comment phrase with backticks as command substitution, then hit unbound variable from scorecard-template.md. Changes reverted.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the richer metric without backticks in comments or strings, then rerun. This was a script quoting bug, not a bad metric idea.

description: Rerun persisted richer quality metric after previous quoting crash

asi.hypothesis: The persisted richer metric script may already be valid after rollback state settled, so rerunning should establish the new baseline.
asi.rollback_reason: Script still crashes with unbound variable `scorecard`; likely a backtick command substitution remains in the printf/comment generated by an earlier edit or the shell is executing a malformed line not visible from the truncated read.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect autoresearch.sh with line numbers and literal characters, then replace the cache_field_count grep with separate file arguments and no risky quoting/backticks.

description: Fix richer quality scorer for cache-accounting metrics

asi.hypothesis: Summing cache-related matches with grep -o and wc will fix the richer quality metric and establish a non-crashing baseline that tracks Claude Bridge cache-accounting completeness.
asi.learned: The previous crash was caused by grep -c across multiple files returning filename-prefixed counts like scorecard-template.md:1, which broke numeric comparison under set -u.
asi.cache_stats_monitoring: Current artifacts contain 12 cache-field references and 11 runbook cache mentions, enough to max the new quality rubric.

description: Add complete Claude Bridge cache columns to scorecard template

asi.hypothesis: Adding uncached input and output token columns to the scorecard makes Anthropic cache accounting via claude-bridge practically complete for scenario scoring.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary quality_score was unchanged at the saturated best of 120 despite cache_field_count increasing from 12 to 14, so autoresearch rules require discard.
asi.next_action_hint: The richer quality metric is also saturated. Either execute the actual A/B eval, or reinitialize with a metric that rewards table schema completeness beyond the current capped cache_field_count threshold.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Validation

description: Switch scorer to validation-focused metric for runnable eval artifacts

asi.hypothesis: A validation-focused metric will avoid saturated completeness scoring by measuring executable scripts and markdown table shape.
asi.rollback_reason: The grep -c multi-file output bug reappeared for table_header_count, producing filename-prefixed counts that broke numeric comparison under set -u.
asi.next_action_hint: Use grep -Eh ... | wc -l for table_header_count, same as the earlier cache count fix, then rerun.

description: Fix validation scorer table counting

asi.hypothesis: Counting markdown table rows with grep -h piped to wc avoids filename-prefixed multi-file counts and establishes a runnable validation metric.
asi.learned: Validation scorer now passes and confirms both helper scripts are executable, table rows are present, and Claude Bridge cache references remain tracked.
asi.bug_pattern: Avoid grep -c across multiple files when the result must be numeric; grep prefixes filenames and breaks arithmetic comparisons.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Artifact Gate

description: Switch to artifact gate metric with syntax and table consistency checks

asi.hypothesis: A stricter gate metric will reveal practical run-readiness issues not captured by saturated completeness metrics.
asi.learned: Scripts pass bash syntax and are executable, but markdown table consistency fails; next iteration should inspect/fix table column mismatch rather than add content.
asi.next_focus: Fix table_consistency_ok for scorecard-template.md and findings-template.md.

description: Normalize scorecard table row column counts

asi.hypothesis: Scorecard body rows missing cache cells caused table_consistency_ok=0; rewriting the scorecard table with matching row widths should raise gate_score.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged; table_consistency_ok remains 0, likely because the scorer incorrectly requires every table in a file to have the same width, while findings-template.md intentionally has multiple independent table widths.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix the scorer to validate each markdown table block independently instead of one expected width per whole file.
asi.evidence: Pre-run inspection showed scorecard rows had 17 pipes vs 19 in header; after fix the gate still failed, pointing to scorer logic across multiple tables.

description: Validate markdown table consistency per contiguous table block

asi.hypothesis: The table consistency gate is failing because findings-template.md has multiple independent table widths; validating contiguous table blocks independently should pass if tables themselves are consistent.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric unchanged and table_consistency_ok remains 0, so at least one contiguous table still has real column mismatch or the block reset logic is insufficient for markdown separators.
asi.next_action_hint: Run a diagnostic that reports file/line/pipe counts per table block after applying the scorer logic, then fix the actual mismatching table rows.
asi.evidence: Changing the scorer alone did not alter table_consistency_ok, so there is still a real table mismatch in current artifacts.

description: Fix scorecard table consistency

asi.hypothesis: Combining the persisted per-table-block scorer with fixed scorecard row widths will resolve the actual markdown table gate failure.
asi.learned: table_consistency_ok is now 1 and gate_score improved from 110 to 120; the remaining artifacts are syntactically runnable and table-consistent.
asi.evidence: Diagnostic showed scorecard header/separator had 19 pipes while scenario rows had 17; adding two missing empty cells per row fixed the gate.

description: Prefer local pi-test.sh launcher in tmux helper scripts

asi.hypothesis: Using the repo-local pi-test.sh when available makes tmux capture scripts more faithful to source-checkout Pi testing than always invoking pi on PATH.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary gate_score unchanged at saturated best 120, so autoresearch rules require discard even though the tweak may be operationally useful.
asi.next_action_hint: Current gate metric is maxed. Reinitialize with an execution-smoke metric that actually runs capture-startup.sh briefly, or start the manual A/B eval.
asi.overfit_check: This was not scorer padding; it addressed a real runbook reliability concern from repo AGENTS testing guidance.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Smoke Readiness

description: Switch to smoke readiness metric for eval helpers

asi.hypothesis: An execution-smoke metric will better reflect whether Luke can run the eval helpers by checking tmux and a Pi launcher in addition to artifact consistency.
asi.learned: Smoke readiness passes: tmux is available, a Pi launcher is available, scripts are executable and syntax-valid, tables are consistent, and cache accounting remains covered.
asi.overfit_check: This metric checks real local prerequisites rather than adding content to satisfy previous saturated rubrics.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Launcher Readiness

description: Prefer repo-local pi-test launcher in tmux helpers

asi.hypothesis: For source-checkout Pi evals, tmux helpers should prefer the repo-local pi-test.sh launcher when available and fall back to pi on PATH; scoring this explicitly improves real run readiness.
asi.learned: Both helper scripts now contain local launcher fallback logic, pass syntax checks, remain executable, and preserve table/cache readiness.
asi.overfit_check: This is tied to repo AGENTS guidance for testing Pi interactive mode via ./pi-test.sh, not benchmark padding.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Helper Dry Run

description: Add dry-run smoke mode for tmux eval helpers

asi.hypothesis: A dry-run mode lets the eval harness verify helper launch commands and prompt injection cheaply without opening an interactive Pi session.
asi.learned: Both tmux helper scripts now support PI_AGENT_EVAL_DRY_RUN=1 and the autoresearch scorer confirms dry-run launch/prompt output works.
asi.overfit_check: This directly improves repeated manual eval reliability and avoids expensive interactive automation.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Dry Run Docs

description: Document dry-run smoke checks for eval helpers

asi.hypothesis: Dry-run helper support is more useful if discoverable in README and runbook, so scoring documentation references improves operator readiness without running expensive evals.
asi.learned: README and runbook now include PI_AGENT_EVAL_DRY_RUN commands; scorer confirms six dry-run documentation references and dry-run helper execution still passes.
asi.overfit_check: This documents an existing cheap validation path for humans; it is not just adding tokens to inflate artifact completeness.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Dry Run Leak Check

description: Add dry-run tmux leak check to scorer

asi.hypothesis: Dry-run mode should be verified not to leave pi-agent-eval tmux sessions behind.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric line was malformed with stray `},{`, so run_experiment could not parse dry_run_leak_score even though secondary leak check passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Remove the stray characters after the primary printf line and rerun; expected dry_run_leak_score should include base readiness, dry_run_ok, docs, and no-leak bonus.
asi.learned: dry_run_no_leak=1 in the malformed run, so the underlying leak-check idea is sound.

description: Add dry-run tmux leak check to eval scorer

asi.hypothesis: Dry-run mode should be verified not to leave pi-agent-eval tmux sessions behind, since repeated eval runs would otherwise accumulate stale sessions.
asi.learned: dry_run_no_leak=1 and dry_run_leak_score=170; the cheap helper smoke path now validates launch output, prompt output, documentation, and tmux session cleanup behavior.
asi.overfit_check: This checks an operational failure mode for repeated tmux-based evals, not benchmark content.

name: Pi Agent Tool A/B Eval Resume Limit
